page: Google Cloud Platform c2d-highmem-112 is a memory-optimized server instance powered by the AMD EPYC 7B13 processor. It features 112 dedicated vCPUs mapped to 56 physical cores with multithreading enabled. The system is equipped with 896 GB of system memory, providing a high memory-to-core ratio of 8.0 GB per vCPU. The x86_64 architecture supports instruction sets such as AVX, AES, VAES, and SHA_NI, though it lacks AVX-512 and AMX extensions. This instance does not include local storage or GPU accelerators.

Benchmark results show top-tier performance in multi-core processing and memory subsystem throughput. The instance achieves top 10% rankings in stress-ng multi-core CPU tests, Passmark CPU physics, and Passmark memory scores. Memory bandwidth is highly efficient, reaching 7672.69 GB/sec for cached small-block reads and maintaining strong transfer rates for large-block operations. In database operations, the server demonstrates high throughput, executing 120.12 million operations per second in Redis SET benchmarks. However, single-threaded performance and text processing fall into the average tier, and text generation for large 70B LLM models is weak at 2.75 tokens per second.

From a cost-efficiency perspective, the c2d-highmem-112 offers high resource density for memory-bound workloads without the added expense of GPU hardware. The lack of bundled local storage requires reliance on network-attached storage, which is a key architectural tradeoff. This configuration is highly suited for large-scale in-memory databases, memory-intensive scientific simulations, software compilation, and high-throughput web serving. It is less suitable for large-scale LLM text generation or workloads requiring local high-speed NVMe drives.
description: The Google Cloud Platform c2d-highmem-112 is a memory-optimized server configured with 112 dedicated vCPUs and 896 GB of RAM, powered by an AMD EPYC 7B13 processor. Operating on the x86_64 architecture, it delivers top-tier multi-core CPU performance and exceptional memory bandwidth, as evidenced by top 10% scores in stress-ng multi-core and Passmark memory benchmarks. It excels in memory-intensive operations, achieving 120.12 million operations per second in Redis SET benchmarks. Tradeoffs include average single-threaded performance, weak text generation speeds for large 70B LLM models, and the absence of local storage or GPU accelerators. This instance is qualitatively cost-efficient for high-density memory workloads, making it highly suitable for in-memory databases, large-scale software compilation, and memory-bound enterprise applications.
og_description: Google Cloud Platform c2d-highmem-112 is a memory-optimized server with 112 dedicated AMD EPYC vCPUs and 896 GB RAM, delivering top-tier multi-core performance and high memory bandwidth for enterprise workloads.
meta_description: Google Cloud Platform c2d-highmem-112 features 112 dedicated AMD EPYC vCPUs and 896 GB RAM, optimized for memory-intensive enterprise workloads.
tagline: A high-density, memory-optimized server featuring dedicated AMD EPYC processors for demanding in-memory databases and multi-threaded enterprise workloads.
bullet_points: Powered by AMD EPYC 7B13 processors with 112 dedicated vCPUs and 56 physical cores.
Equipped with 896 GB of RAM, providing a high 8.0 GB per vCPU memory ratio.
Top-tier multi-core CPU performance and memory bandwidth in benchmark testing.
Achieves 120.12 million operations per second in Redis SET benchmarks.
Lacks local storage and GPU accelerators, requiring network-attached storage.
Best suited for in-memory databases, software compilation, and memory-bound workloads.
